title: semecarpus cuneifolius
intro: Semecarpus cuneifolius, also known as the markab tree, is a plant species native to India and Sri Lanka. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly the sap of its fruit, which contains a resin called jingili or markab resin, with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Semecarpus cuneifolius include its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its use as an insecticide. The herbal preparations of Semecarpus cuneifolius include the use of its resin in traditional medicine, which can be made into a paste or mixed with other herbs to create a treatment for various ailments.
